problem: When a class of math students lined up for a class picture on Picture Day, they found that when they stood in rows of four there was one person left over. When they formed rows of five students, there were two extra students, and when they lined up in rows of seven, there were three students left over. What is the fewest number of students possible in this class?

classified_questions.question: How many students were in the class?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the conditions that the number of students in the class must satisfy?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How did you use the first two pieces of information to determine the possible values for the number of students?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How did you check the values of the number of students that satisfy the first two conditions?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the significance of finding an integer that satisfies all three conditions?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: How can the Chinese Remainder Theorem be used to simplify the solution for this problem?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there other problems that can be solved using similar methods? If so, can you provide an example?
classified_questions.label: e
